scenario: Iris Telco models a Camunda 8.8 service activation workflow that handles 9000 customers per day. The architect catalogs which task type icons the BPMN coverage page lists with an implementation marker and which appear without one before approving the modeling palette.
question: Which task type icon in the BPMN coverage page is shown without an implementation marker in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The service task icon on the BPMN coverage tasks section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The business rule task icon on the BPMN coverage tasks section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The script task icon on the BPMN coverage tasks section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8
The instantiated receive task icon on the BPMN coverage tasks section appears without an implementation marker for execution in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the service task icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes service tasks through job workers.

b.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the business rule task icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes business rule tasks for DMN or worker implementations.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN coverage page lists the script task icon with the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes script tasks via FEEL or job workers.

d.text: Correct. The BPMN coverage page lists the instantiated receive task icon without an implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 does not execute the instantiated receive task variant.

explanation: In the BPMN coverage tasks section, service, user, receive, send, business rule, script, manual, and undefined task icons carry the implementation marker, while the instantiated receive task icon appears without one.
